question.text: In estate planning, intestate succession refers to:

answer.text: Distribution of assets according to a registered Will
Distribution of assets according to applicable succession laws when no valid Will exists
Transfer of assets through a family trust
Sale of assets by the court and distribution of proceeds

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Intestate succession occurs when a person dies without a valid Will. In such cases, assets are distributed according to the applicable succession laws (Hindu Succession Act, Indian Succession Act, etc.) based on the deceased's religion. **The Trap:** Option A describes testate succession (with a Will). Many confuse the two terms. Intestate literally means 'without a testament/will'. **Cross-Exam Context:** Succession laws are covered in detail in Series X-B (estate planning module) and introduced in Series XVII for retirement advisers. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The default succession rules may not align with the client's wishes. Always recommend making a Will to avoid intestate succession and ensure assets go to intended beneficiaries.
